threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus stops and everyone suddenly jumps off even if it’s not their stop. Just wait, you'll get the hang of it.
Last night on the 59, a bloke serenaded us with old Motown hits. Only in Brixton would you find an impromptu karaoke session at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid selling homemade lemonade on the corner of Weserstraße, while his mom chatted with the neighbors. That’s when it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a döner at 3 AM after clubbing, then ice cream from a shop that takes credit cards but not cash. Classic Berlin chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone is local when they can spot a tourist by their confused face when a bus is late for the third time that week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 last week near Civic Center, the driver had to dodge a guy in a full dinosaur suit. Just another day in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often assume Gràcia's plazas are for chatting, but most locals just grab a quick coffee and dash off. It's not a lounge, it's a pit stop.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map while everyone else seamlessly weaves underground. It's that look of bewilderment that gives them away.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'sandwich' rather than a 'butty' and think ‘chippy’ is just a kid's menu item.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and overheard two guys debating the best chicken shops. Turns out the local debates are just as intense at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I found a half-broken piano on the street, surrounded by kids pretending to be a band. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous street parties breaking out for no reason at all—last weekend, a DJ set up by the canal and folks just danced like it was normal.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who's local by their patience at bus stops, even when Muni's delayed because of a dog on the tracks. Tourists fume; locals shrug.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, I watched a guy with a suitcase hop on a Muni just as a flock of pigeons scattered—perfect chaos. Just another day in Civic Center.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can enjoy a quiet meal in the plaza, but every evening it transforms into a vibrant concert of kids, dogs, and street music.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the bus schedules; locals know they’re just suggestions, not timetables.
